text.title: Nginx Production LB Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in production Nginx LB configs. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Under load test, the LB host runs out of ephemeral ports (`TIME_WAIT` ≈ 30k). Latency degrades; CPU spent in kernel `connect()` calls.
antipattern.root-cause: `upstream` block lacks `keepalive 32;` AND `location` block lacks `proxy_http_version 1.1; proxy_set_header Connection "";`. Every request opens + tears down a fresh TCP connection to upstream.
antipattern.fix: Add the keepalive directives to upstream + location. Repeat the load test; `TIME_WAIT` count drops 10–100× and latency falls.

antipattern.symptom: One abusive client floods `/api/login`; upstream auth pool saturates; legitimate users get 5xx for 10 minutes until WAF rules are pushed.
antipattern.root-cause: No `limit_req_zone` declared; no `limit_req` on the login endpoint.
antipattern.fix: Declare `limit_req_zone $binary_remote_addr zone=login_per_ip:10m rate=5r/s;` and apply `limit_req zone=login_per_ip burst=10 nodelay;` on `/api/login`. Re-test: 429s replace upstream saturation.

antipattern.symptom: 16-core LB host serves at 1/16 of capacity; `top` shows one nginx worker at 100 % CPU while the other 15 cores sit idle.
antipattern.root-cause: Author copied an old `worker_processes 1;` snippet into production.
antipattern.fix: Change to `worker_processes auto;`. Add `worker_rlimit_nofile 65535;`. Restart; the host now uses all cores.
